title: The Gilded Ferry
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A former river detective boards a private ferry where every passenger links to an old dockside murder.
long_description:
The Gilded Ferry follows Silas Wren, an adult former river detective, through a fogbound Victorian river as a former river detective boards a private ferry where every passenger links to an old dockside murder. The inquiry moves through offices, sealed rooms, public hearings, and physical evidence, keeping its suspense grounded in work, speech, and consequence.

As Ada Pike, Nell Brack, and Engineer Hugo Saye help test the record, Magistrate Lowell Greve tries to turn delay into truth. The novel is clean adult suspense about institutions, memory, and the cost of naming what everyone was paid not to see.
